description: Supply chain security, SBOM, provenance attestation, and compliance packaging for SDK releases

sbom_generation.verification: SBOM must list all direct and transitive dependencies with exact versions
sbom_generation.storage: SBOM artifacts stored alongside release packages and linked in release notes

signing_policy.requirement: All release artifacts MUST be signed before publishing
signing_policy.key_management: Signing keys stored in CI/CD secrets vault (not in repo)
signing_policy.key_rotation: Signing keys rotated annually or on compromise

checksum_policy.distribution: Checksums published as .sha256 / .sha512 files alongside release artifacts

artifact_signing_and_checksum.integrity_check_doc:
Consumers can verify package integrity by:
1. Download package + checksum file
2. Run: sha256sum -c package.sha256
3. Verify Sigstore attestation: cosign verify-blob --bundle attestation.json package.tar.gz

pre_release_gates.policy: FAIL on critical/high CVEs. Medium CVEs must be triaged within 7 days.
pre_release_gates.bypass: Only with written security-team approval and mitigation plan

pre_release_gates.policy: FAIL on GPL, AGPL, SSPL, or unknown licenses
FAIL on any detected secret (API keys, tokens, passwords)

pre_release_gates.scope: SDK source code only (not test fixtures)

security_scanning_gates.pipeline_enforcement: All gates run in CI. Release pipeline BLOCKS on any unresolved critical security or license violation.

advisory_process: 1. Receive vulnerability report via disclosure channel
2. Triage: assign severity (CVSS), identify affected SDK versions
4. Release patched versions for all affected SDKs simultaneously
6. Notify consumers via release notes + advisory feed
7. Update compatibility matrix with minimum safe versions

steps: 1. Install SDK package from official registry
2. Verify package signature using ecosystem-specific tools (see checksum_policy.verification_commands)
3. For additional assurance, verify Sigstore attestation using cosign
4. Compare SBOM against your organizational allowed-dependency list
5. Monitor advisory feed for vulnerability notifications
